calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Continued To Mar-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MERCY HOUSING MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC. AND MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for violation of Labor Code 232.5, second cause of action for violation of Labor Code 1102.5, third cause of action for violation of Labor Code 6310, fifth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy in Labor Code 232.5, sixth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy in Labor Code 1102.5, seventh cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy in Labor Code 6310, and ninth cause of action wrongful termination in violation of public policy in Labor Code 6400; and b) sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for violation of Civil Code 52.1 (Bane Act) and eighth cause of action for wrongful termination violation of public policy in the Bane Act. Plaintiff Maria Ballesteros has not pled sufficient facts to state any viable cause of action. Ms. Ballesteros has not alleged any nexus between her applying for and obtaining a restraining order and defendants' decisions to suspend and terminate her employment. Moreover, Ms. Ballesteros has not alleged any facts showing that she is covered by the cited statutes. For example, with respect to the Labor Code 6310 claims, Ms. Ballesteros does not allege any facts that she made or the defendants expected that she would make a workplace safety complaint. Except for her Bane Act claims, if she can do so in good faith, Ms. Ballesteros is given leave to amend her complaint to correct the deficiencies in all of her claims. Moreover, if she can do so in good faith and in compliance with this order, Ms. Ballesteros may also include in her amended complaint a claim based on CCP 527.6 and 527.8. As to her Bane Act claims, Ms. Ballesteros has not alleged any conduct of defendants that violates that statute nor does it appear reasonably possible that she can do so and thus granting her leave to amend as to her Bane Act claims is unwarranted. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
